line: First Baptist Church: Rev. W. F. Harper, pastor. The First Baptist Church was organized in 1872. Rev. J. C. Post, was the first pastor. In 1873 a brick chapel 30 by 50 feet was built and dedicated at a cost of $3,000. After a very successful two-years pastorage Elder Post resigned and the church was without preaching for several months. Rev. W. F. File supplied for six months. He was followed by Rev. J. F. Davis who served the church with acceptance a little more than one year. After the resignation of Elder Davis there was another interim of several months in the pastorate. Rev. A. L. Vail came in 1879 and remained two years. Elder Vail resigned in the spring of 1881 and several months followed without pastoral leadership. In October 1881, Elder W. J Sandefur came as supply for six months. The present pastorate commenced April 1st, 1882, Rev. W. F. Harper having been called by the church some months before. When he arrived upon the field he found an enrollment of 79 members and a Sabbath-school with an attendance of about 70. The present church membership is 350. In 1883-4 the audience room was added to the chapel at a cost of $5,500. Present value of church property is $20,000. Pastor W. F. Harper; deacons, D. W. Smith, Peter Glunt, J. T. Holmes, A. Ray; trustees: J. L. Dyer, E. J. Foster, Jas. McComb; treasurer, E. J. Foster; clerk, A. D. Phelps. Sunday-school superintendent, D. W. Smith.
